note: The vast central square is flat and open, easy to roll across, ringed by the Cathedral, National Palace and Templo Mayor. A good accessible orientation point; surrounding historic-centre sidewalks vary.
World-class museum in Chapultepec; impressive and accessible with ramps/lifts and wide galleries. A must-see and a reliable accessible day. Vast - pace yourself.
Huge city park with the museum, zoo, lake and wide paths; largely flat and accessible, great for families. The park is easy; the hilltop Castle within it is the hard part.

Hilltop castle with city views; reaching it involves a steep climb up the hill (a road-train/vehicle assist may help) and the historic building has steps. Views are great but access is genuinely tough.

Casa Azul in Coyoacan; better than expected for a historic house - ramps, an elevator, Braille labels and FREE disabled entry cover the ground floor and garden, but a small upstairs portion is stairs and rooms are narrow/tight. Book online well ahead.
Charming colonial neighbourhood around Casa Azul; one of the more accessible districts but with cobblestone streets, a lively market and plazas. Atmospheric, navigable with care.
Iconic art-nouveau/deco cultural palace with murals; accessible with lifts, and the surrounding Alameda Central park is flat. A strong central accessible stop.
Aztec ruins beside the Cathedral; the museum is accessible but the archaeological site itself has raised walkways, ramps in parts and some uneven/stepped sections over the ruins. Partly manageable.

Trendy leafy districts with parks (Parque Mexico/Espana), cafes and European vibe; among the more accessible areas to stroll, though sidewalk/curb-cut quality is inconsistent. Uber is the easy way between sights.
Famous canal boats (colourful trajineras); boarding the flat-bottomed boats can be managed with assistance from the dock but is not step-free. A fun, festive experience if you have help.
